title: NJU CG2020 — 2D Computer Graphics System
summary: A 2D graphics course project (NJU CG2020) that implements fundamental rendering algorithms in Python. The core algorithms module (cg_algorithms.py) provides all 2D primitives, transforms, clipping, and polygon fill; the CLI module (cg_cli.py) reads text instruction files, maintains a scene, and outputs BMP images; and the PyQt frontend (cg_gui.py) provides an interactive canvas that dispatches to those algorithms.
instructionPreview: This is a computer-graphics coursework project that implements fundamental 2D graphics algorithms. The system reads drawing commands from text instruction files, processes them through a rendering pipeline, outputs BMP images, and also ships with an interactive PyQt drawing frontend.
instruction:
This is a computer-graphics coursework project that implements fundamental
2D graphics algorithms. The system reads drawing commands from text instruction files,
processes them through a rendering pipeline, outputs BMP images, and also ships
with an interactive PyQt drawing frontend.

Your working directory is `/workspace`. The project code is in `/workspace/CG_demo/`.
You need to implement all the modules and features listed below so that all tests pass.

## Modules to Implement

- **cg_algorithms** (`CG_demo/cg_algorithms.py`): Core graphics algorithms module.
  Implements all 2D primitives: line drawing (DDA, Bresenham), polygon rendering,
  ellipse drawing via midpoint algorithm, curve generation (Bezier and B-spline),
  geometric transformations (translate, rotate, scale), line clipping
  (Cohen-Sutherland, Liang-Barsky), polygon clipping, and scanline polygon fill
  using edge tables. Only the `math` library is allowed as a dependency.
  (~13 functions to implement)

- **cg_cli** (`CG_demo/cg_cli.py`): Command-line rendering pipeline. Parses text
  instruction files containing drawing commands (resetCanvas, saveCanvas, setColor,
  drawLine, drawPolygon, drawEllipse, drawCurve, translate, rotate, scale, clip),
  maintains a dictionary of graphics items with their properties, renders all items
  onto a numpy canvas with Y-axis flipping (screen coordinates), and saves output
  as BMP images. (~3 functions to implement)

- **cg_gui** (`CG_demo/cg_gui.py`): Interactive PyQt frontend. The window/widget
  scaffold is already provided, but the course-specific logic in `MyCanvas` and
  `MyItem` is hollowed out: interaction-mode state management, selection/edit
  operations, undo/copy-paste/save hooks, mouse-driven drawing / transform /
  clipping behavior, and item painting / bounds that dispatch to `cg_algorithms`.
  (~18 methods across `MyCanvas` and `MyItem`)

## Test-Coupled Signatures

The following functions in `cg_algorithms.py` are called by the rendering pipeline
and must be implemented with these exact signatures:

- `draw_line(p_list, algorithm)` — returns pixel coordinate list for a line segment using the specified algorithm ('DDA' or 'Bresenham')
- `draw_polygon(p_list, algorithm)` — returns pixel coordinates for a closed polygon
- `draw_part_polygon(p_list, algorithm)` — returns pixel coordinates for an open polyline (no closing edge)
- `draw_ellipse(p_list)` — returns pixel coordinates for an ellipse given its bounding box
- `Bezier_point(n, t, control_point)` — computes a single point on a Bezier curve at parameter t
- `deboox_cox(i, k, u)` — evaluates the De Boor-Cox basis function for B-spline computation
- `draw_curve(p_list, algorithm)` — returns pixel coordinates for a curve ('Bezier' or 'B-spline')
- `translate(p_list, dx, dy)` — applies translation transform to a point list
- `rotate(p_list, x, y, r)` — applies clockwise rotation by r degrees around center (x, y)
- `scale(p_list, x, y, s)` — applies scaling by factor s around center (x, y)
- `clip(p_list, x_min, y_min, x_max, y_max, algorithm)` — clips a line segment to a rectangle ('Cohen-Sutherland' or 'Liang-Barsky')
- `polygon_clip(p_list, x_min, y_min, x_max, y_max)` — clips a polygon to a rectangle
- `polygon_fill(p_list)` — fills a polygon using scanline algorithm with edge tables

The following functions in `cg_cli.py` are used by the CLI entry point:

- `render_canvas(item_dict, width, height)` — renders all graphics items onto a white numpy canvas (height x width x 3), applying Y-axis flip for screen coordinates
- `parse_command(line, item_dict, pen_color, width, height)` — parses a tokenized command, updates item_dict/pen_color/dimensions, returns (item_dict, pen_color, width, height, save_info)
- `run_cli(input_file, output_dir)` — reads the instruction file line by line, processes commands, and saves output BMP images

The interactive frontend in `cg_gui.py` should preserve the existing PyQt scaffold
while implementing the hollowed course logic in these methods:

- `MyCanvas.start_translate(...)`, `MyCanvas.start_rotate(...)`, `MyCanvas.start_scale(...)`, `MyCanvas.start_clip(...)`, `MyCanvas.start_copy(...)`, `MyCanvas.start_paste(...)`, `MyCanvas.start_cancel(...)`, `MyCanvas.save_canvas(...)`, `MyCanvas.save(...)`, `MyCanvas.start_undo(...)`, `MyCanvas.start_clip_polygon(...)`
- `MyCanvas.selection_changed(...)`, `MyCanvas.mousePressEvent(...)`, `MyCanvas.mouseMoveEvent(...)`, `MyCanvas.mouseReleaseEvent(...)`, `MyCanvas.wheelEvent(...)`
- `MyItem.paint(...)`, `MyItem.boundingRect(...)`

moduleDag.description: A 2D graphics course project (NJU CG2020) that implements fundamental rendering algorithms in Python. The core algorithms module (cg_algorithms.py) provides all 2D primitives, transforms, clipping, and polygon fill; the CLI module (cg_cli.py) reads text instruction files, maintains a scene, and outputs BMP images; and the PyQt frontend (cg_gui.py) provides an interactive canvas that dispatches to those algorithms.

nodes.label: Line Drawing (DDA & Bresenham)
nodes.description: Implements draw_line using DDA and Bresenham algorithms, producing a pixel coordinate list for a line segment. Foundation for all polygon drawing functions.

nodes.label: Ellipse Drawing (Midpoint Algorithm)
nodes.description: Implements draw_ellipse via the midpoint circle generation algorithm. Takes a bounding rectangle (two corner points) and returns pixel coordinates for the ellipse outline. Completely independent of other drawing algorithms.

nodes.label: Curve Generation (Bezier & B-spline)
nodes.description: Implements Bezier_point (de Casteljau recursive interpolation), deboox_cox (De Boor-Cox basis function for B-spline), and draw_curve which dispatches to either algorithm. The two helpers are consumed internally by draw_curve and must be implemented first.

nodes.label: Geometric Transforms (translate, rotate, scale)
nodes.description: Implements translate, rotate (clockwise by degrees around a center), and scale as pure point-list transformations. Each takes a list of (x, y) control points and returns a transformed list. Only the math library is used.

nodes.label: Line Clipping (Cohen-Sutherland & Liang-Barsky)
nodes.description: Implements clip for clipping a line segment to a rectangular window using either the Cohen-Sutherland outcode algorithm or the Liang-Barsky parametric algorithm. Independent of drawing primitives.

nodes.label: Polygon Clipping (Sutherland-Hodgman)
nodes.description: Implements polygon_clip to clip a polygon against an axis-aligned rectangle using four successive edge-clipping passes (Sutherland-Hodgman approach). Independent of other clipping and drawing functions.

nodes.label: Scanline Polygon Fill
nodes.description: Implements polygon_fill using the scanline edge-table algorithm. Builds a New Edge Table (NET) and Active Edge Table (AET) using the Node linked-list class, then fills interior pixels scanline-by-scanline. Independent of other algorithms.

nodes.label: Polygon Drawing (draw_polygon & draw_part_polygon)
nodes.description: Implements draw_polygon (closed polygon, connects last vertex back to first) and draw_part_polygon (open polyline). Both iterate over vertex pairs and delegate each segment to draw_line, so line_drawing must be complete first.

nodes.label: CLI Command Parser (parse_command)
nodes.description: Implements parse_command which tokenizes a single instruction line and dispatches to handlers for resetCanvas, saveCanvas, setColor, drawLine, drawPolygon, drawEllipse, drawCurve, translate, rotate, scale, and clip. Calls alg.translate, alg.rotate, alg.scale, and alg.clip, so geometric_transforms and line_clipping must be implemented first.

nodes.label: CLI Canvas Renderer (render_canvas)
nodes.description: Implements render_canvas, which iterates over item_dict and calls the appropriate drawing primitive from cg_algorithms for each item type (line, polygon, ellipse, curve), writing pixel colors to a white numpy canvas with Y-axis flip. Requires all drawing primitives to be implemented.

nodes.label: Interactive GUI Frontend (MyCanvas & MyItem)
nodes.description: Implements the course-specific logic inside the provided PyQt scaffold: canvas interaction modes, selection/edit operations, undo/copy-paste, save hooks, mouse-driven drawing and transforms, plus MyItem.paint / boundingRect for dispatching to cg_algorithms. Depends on the drawing, transform, clipping, and polygon-fill units.

nodes.label: CLI Entry Point (run_cli)
nodes.description: Implements run_cli which opens an instruction file, calls parse_command for each line, and saves BMP images via PIL whenever a saveCanvas command is encountered. Depends on both parse_command and render_canvas being complete.

edges.label: draw_polygon and draw_part_polygon call draw_line for each consecutive vertex pair
render_canvas calls alg.draw_line for items of type 'line'
render_canvas calls alg.draw_polygon for items of type 'polygon'
MyItem.paint dispatches line items to alg.draw_line
MyItem.paint dispatches polygon previews/final polygons to alg.draw_part_polygon / alg.draw_polygon
render_canvas calls alg.draw_ellipse for items of type 'ellipse'
MyItem.paint dispatches ellipse items to alg.draw_ellipse
render_canvas calls alg.draw_curve for items of type 'curve'
MyItem.paint and MyCanvas curve interactions dispatch to alg.draw_curve
parse_command calls alg.translate, alg.rotate, alg.scale for transform commands
MyCanvas mouse interactions dispatch translate / rotate / scale edits through cg_algorithms
parse_command calls alg.clip for the 'clip' command
MyCanvas clip mode dispatches line edits to alg.clip
MyCanvas polygon clip mode dispatches polygon edits to alg.polygon_clip
MyItem.paint dispatches filled polygons to alg.polygon_fill
run_cli drives parse_command which internally calls render_canvas on saveCanvas
run_cli calls parse_command on every line of the instruction file
